b b b
This is the AND pattern. But the level still isn't complete.
Let me think about this more systematically. I've tried many patterns:

3. Top-left pattern: b R b / R ? b / b R R
4. Top-right pattern: b R b / R ? R / b R b
5. All blue: b b b / b ? b / b b b
6. AND pattern: b b b / R ? b / b b b (current)
None of these completed the level. Maybe I need to think about this differently.
Let me look at the center cell patterns again. Each reference grid has a unique center pattern. Maybe the goal is to match a specific center pattern, and the outer cells are just a side effect.
Actually, wait. Let me reconsider the problem. Maybe the goal is to make the bottom-right grid match ALL three reference grids simultaneously in some way. Or maybe the goal is to create a pattern that's the "inverse" of one of the references.
Let me try the inverse of the top-left pattern:

Let me try this.
Actually, let me try a completely different approach. Let me enumerate all 2^8 = 256 possible patterns and see if any of them completes the level. But that's too many to try manually.
Wait, maybe the goal is about the center cell, not the outer cells. Let me look at the center cell patterns more carefully.
Top-left center: ggWWgg / ggWWgg / WWRRgg / WWRRgg / ggWWWW / ggWWWW
Top-right center: ggWWgg / ggWWgg / WWRRWW / WWRRWW / ggWWgg / ggWWgg
Bottom-left center: WWgggg / WWgggg / WWRRWW / WWRRWW / ggggWW / ggggWW
Bottom-right center: WWgggg / WWgggg / WWRRWW / WWRRWW / WWgggg / WWgggg
The bottom-right center currently matches none of the reference centers. Maybe I need to change the center cell's pattern to match one of the references.
But how do I change the center cell? Clicking on the outer cells changes their color. Maybe clicking on the center cell changes its sub-pattern.
Let me try clicking on different parts of the center cell. The center cell is at rows 44-49, cols 44-49.
Actually, I just realized something. The center cell might not be clickable at all. Maybe the center cell pattern is determined by the outer cell pattern, and I need to find the right outer cell pattern that produces the desired center pattern.
But earlier, when I changed the outer cells, the center didn't change. So the center is independent.
Let me try clicking on the center cell at different positions to see if I can change its pattern. Let me try clicking at the center of the center cell (47, 47).
